questions.question: What is the correct syntax for an HTML comment?
questions.explanation: HTML comments use the <!-- --> syntax. The other formats are used in JavaScript, CSS, and Python respectively.

questions.question: What happens to HTML comments when a page is rendered in the browser?
choices: They are displayed as plain text
They are hidden from view but remain in the source code
They are completely removed from the DOM
questions.explanation: HTML comments are not rendered visually in the browser, but they remain in the page source code and can be viewed by inspecting the source.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid use case for conditional comments in HTML?
choices: Targeting specific versions of Internet Explorer
questions.explanation: Conditional comments were used to target specific versions of Internet Explorer, allowing developers to include IE-specific CSS or HTML.

questions.question: Which sequence of characters is NOT allowed inside an HTML comment?
questions.explanation: The double hyphen (--) sequence is not allowed inside HTML comments because it can be misinterpreted by the parser as the end of the comment.

questions.question: An HTML comment begins with <!-- and ends with ___.
questions.explanation: HTML comments are enclosed between <!-- and -->. Both the opening and closing delimiters are required for a valid comment.

questions.question: Conditional comments were primarily used to target the ___ browser.
questions.explanation: Conditional comments were a feature recognized only by Internet Explorer, allowing developers to serve IE-specific content.
